# cargo-geiger unsafe-dependency baseline (RFC-0041 §5 / RR-29 §4).
#
# PLACEHOLDER — this baseline has NOT been generated by a real `cargo geiger` run. cargo-geiger was
# not installed in the W0 sandbox that authored this file (`command -v cargo-geiger` failed; no
# network install attempted). FLAGGED for follow-up (G2 — never-silent): install cargo-geiger
# (`cargo install cargo-geiger` or `just setup-scan`) and run
# `scripts/checks/geiger-baseline.sh --update` to replace this placeholder with a real baseline
# BEFORE this gate is relied upon to catch a new unsafe dependency (the prime candidate: W2 wiring
# `stacker`/`psm` into `mycelium-stack`, RFC-0041 §4.3).
#
# Known facts as of W0 (`Empirical`, grounded in cited sources — not from a geiger run):
#   - RR-29 §4 / DN-21 §6: the entire first-party workspace `unsafe` surface is confined to
#     crates/mycelium-mlir/src/jit.rs (audited; safety.sh Audit 1 covers it).
#   - `stacker`/`psm` are NOT yet dependencies: `grep -c "stacker\|psm" Cargo.lock` = 0 as of this
#     writing (crates/mycelium-stack/Cargo.toml documents them as a commented-out future option only).
#   - No committed record of THIRD-PARTY dependency unsafe usage exists yet — this file is that
#     record's placeholder, not (yet) a real baseline.
#
# Until `--update` is run for real, `geiger-baseline.sh`'s first genuine run will report every
# currently-unsafe-using third-party dependency as "new" — this is EXPECTED and correct (the
# placeholder baseline is empty of real entries below), not a false alarm.
